etymology: From Proto-Turkic *čerig. Cognate to Southern Altai черӱ (čerü), etc.

from Proto-Turkic *eńe (“mother”), a front vowel parallel to *ana (“mother”).
Cognate with Bashkir инәй (inəy, “mother; address to old woman”), Tatar әни (äni, “mother”), Southern Altai эне (ene, “mother”), Kazakh ене (ene, “mother-in-law”), Yakut ийе (iye, “mother”); Khakas иней (iney, “old woman”), etc.

en: writing, writing system, script, written language
senses: writing, writing system, script, written language

etymology: From Proto-Turkic *kamčï (“whip”), a derivation from *kam- (“to beat, strike down”). Cognate with Karakhanid قَمْجٖى (qamčï̄, “whip”), قَمّاقْ (qammāq, “to beat”).
From Proto-Turkic *yara. Compare to Southern Altai јара (ǰara), etc.
From Arabic طَبَق (ṭabaq, “plate”). Cognate with Bashkir табаҡ (tabaq, “bowl”), Southern Altai табак (tabak, “plate”), Uzbek tovoq (“bowl”), etc.
From Arabic طَبَق (ṭabaq, “plate”). Cognate with Bashkir табаҡ (tabaq, “bowl”), Southern Altai табак (tabak, “plate”), Uzbek tovoq (“bowl”), etc.

From Proto-Common Turkic *bulak. Cognate with Azerbaijani bulaq, Turkish bulak, Uzbek buloq, Uyghur بۇلاق (bulaq), Kumyk булакъ (bulaq), Kazakh бұлақ (būlaq), Southern Altai булак (bulak), Nogai булак (bulak), Bashkir болаҡ (bolaq), Tatar bolaq, Western Yugur pulaq etc. See also Mongolian булаг (bulag).
From Persian شمع (šam'), from Arabic شَمْع (šamʕ, “candle”).
Borrowed from Arabic قَلَم (qalam), from Ancient Greek κάλαμος (kálamos).

Borrowed from Arabic قَلَم (qalam), from Ancient Greek κάλαμος (kálamos). + From Proto-Turkic *sap (handle).
калем (kalem, “pencil”) + сап (sap, “handle”).
Ultimately from Proto-Turkic *kul. Cognates with Azerbaijani qul, Bashkir ҡол (qol), Kazakh құл (qūl), Southern Altai кул (kul), Tatar кол (qol), Turkish kul, Turkmen gul, Uzbek qul.

senses: woodlouse, roly-poly, pillbug, sowbug (any species of suborder Oniscidea)
waiting area (in an airport, after passing security)

etymology: From Persian عيال, from Arabic عَيَال (ʕayāl, “offspring”).
From Proto-Turkic *ügi. Cognate with Kazakh үкі (ükı), Southern Altai ӱкӱ (ükü).
From Proto-Turkic *kudruk (“tail”). Cognate with Old Turkic 𐰴𐰆𐰑𐰺𐰆𐰸 (qudruq, “tail”), Turkish kuyruk (“tail”).
